should_trigger: Design a multiagent orchestration protocol where planner, coder, reviewer, and test agents each have local state and must converge on a final patch without duplicate work.
Our specialist agents keep choosing incompatible tool actions because each only sees local constraints. Use multiagent systems theory to fix the coordination protocol.
We need a bidding or auction-like mechanism for allocating scarce sandbox time across autonomous execution agents with different priorities and expected value.
How should an auto-orchestrator aggregate rankings from several evaluator agents when some agents may be biased or strategically overconfident?
should_not_trigger: Design a single-agent checklist for running tests after a code change.
Explain Kubernetes service discovery for stateless microservices that do not make autonomous decisions.
Debug a database retry loop where there is only one worker and no interaction between decision-making agents.
edge_cases: A system has many workers and queues, but all decisions are made by one central scheduler; trigger only if the workers have local decision authority, private information, or strategic incentives that affect the outcome.
